summary:
The ophthalmic artery is not a branch of the external carotid artery; instead, it originates from the internal carotid artery. All other listed vessels—posterior auricular, occipital, and maxillary—are recognized branches of the external carotid artery.  

\boxed{D}
formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 1 summarized): The ophthalmic artery is not a branch of the external carotid artery; instead, it originates from the internal carotid artery. All other listed vessels—posterior auricular, occipital, and maxillary—are recognized branches of the external carotid artery.  

\boxed{D}. Answer choice: D
